title: Audio & Visual System Structure Description - Overview

blocks.text: Audio & Visual System Structure Description - Overview

The audio and visual system of this model consists of front speakers, rear speakers, audio remote switch, HFT switch, roof antenna, and rear camera as external connection devices, centering on the audio unit.

The audio unit has a 7" WVGA display with capacitive touch panel. Two analog microphones for HFT, an antenna for Bluetooth communication, and an antenna for wireless LAN communication are built into the front panel. The microphones are highly directional with a beam forming function to cut out ambient noise and input audio from the driver's side. The system is also equipped with a smartphone linkage function and video playback function as standard features.

Capacitive touch panels link finger movement to screen operation by detecting the position of the voltage change generated by changing the charge in the touch sensor (capacitive coupling) when a finger, which is an electrical conductor, is brought close to the panel.
USB and HDMI jacks are provided for external audio/video input and smartphone connection.

Compliant with Bluetooth Ver3.0 + EDR, it enables streaming reception of music data, control of Audio playback devices, hands-free phone functions using cell phones, data communication, and phonebook data acquisition.
Wi-Fi Direct-compatible devices can be connected to the audio and navigation unit using a wireless LAN compliant with IEEE802.11b/g/n.
Smartphones can be connected to audio and navigation units to safely use content while driving. Connection methods vary by device and carrier.

The system has a speed-linked volume function that automatically increases or decreases the volume of the audio system according to the vehicle's own speed, controlled by the vehicle speed signal (VSP) from the B-CAN, with the volume increasing as the vehicle speed increases and decreasing as the vehicle speed decreases. The volume amplification can be adjusted in four levels, in the order of LOW, MID, and HIGH, and the speed-linked volume function is turned off when the volume is turned off.

A mode of screen diagnosis is provided as a self-diagnostic function to diagnose the system in the event of a malfunction. With the map screen displayed, press the eject button, power ( The self-diagnostic function is activated by simultaneously pressing and holding down the (∗) button and the HOME button until the diagnostic menu screen appears. The system also has a hardware error code display function specific to audio and navigation units, which can be checked on the diagnostic screen.
If the audio unit detects abnormally high temperatures, the fail-safe function performs the following actions

items.text: Built-in fan activation/deactivation control: When a temperature higher than the appropriate temperature is detected, the fan activation is started, and when the temperature returns to the appropriate temperature, the fan is deactivated.

Display backlight ON/OFF control: When a high display temperature is detected, the display backlight is turned off after an error message is displayed (the system continues to operate). When the temperature returns to an appropriate level, the backlight is turned ON.

blocks.text: A rear camera with a CMOS image sensor having an effective pixel count of 340,000 is provided for checking the rearward direction. To facilitate rearward checking when the vehicle is in reverse (R), the image from the rear camera can be displayed on the audio unit's display when the select lever is in reverse (R). The image captured by the rear camera is sent directly to the audio unit. The image of the rear of the vehicle displayed on the display is inverted so that the image on the left side of the vehicle is displayed on the left side of the display screen and the image on the right side of the vehicle is displayed on the right side of the display screen.
The rear wide camera system displays three different views of the rear camera on the display, and the view can be changed by user operation. The guide lines vary depending on the selected view.
When the rear camera image is displayed, use the CAMERA BIT0 and CAMERA BIT1 signal wires to send a signal from the audio to the rear camera in order to change the view of the rear camera.

When guide lines are displayed in the rear camera image (in the case of Wide View and Normal View), the guide lines change according to the steering wheel angle (based on the steering angle signal from the B-CAN) (when the steering wheel angle is 90° or more).

When a cell phone is connected, the unit functions as a hands-free telephone (HFT); audio input is picked up by the unit's built-in microphone when using HFT, and audio output is provided by the front left speaker.
